verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Now when Providence had delivered us in such an extraordinary manner, who can justly lay any blame upon me, because we did not give ourselves up into the hands of them that sought after us, nor return and present ourselves before them?

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: This would have been plainly to shew ingratitude to the Lord, and to act against His commandment, and in contradiction to the practice of the Saints.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: He who censures me in this matter must presume also to blame the great Apostle Peter, because though he was shut up and guarded by soldiers, he followed the angel that summoned him, and when he had gone forth from the prison and escaped in safety, he did not return and surrender himself, although he heard what Herod had done.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: Let the Arian in his madness censure the Apostle Paul, because when he was let down from the wall and had escaped in safety, he did not change his mind, and return and give himself up; or Moses, because he returned not out of Midian into Egypt, that he might be taken of them that sought after him; or David, because when he was concealed in the cave, he did not discover himself to Saul.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: As also the sons of the prophets remained in their caves, and did not surrender themselves to Ahab.

verses.book: Defence of His Flight. (Apologia de Fuga.)
verses.text: This would have been to act contrary to the commandment, since the Scripture says, ‘Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.’
